verses.text: The burden of Tyre: the ships of Tarshish howl, for the great merchant city is laid waste. Her trade is ruined. The wealth of nations cannot stand against the judgment of God.
The burden of Tyre: the ships of Tarshish howl, for the great merchant city is laid waste. Her trade is ruined. The wealth of nations cannot stand against the judgment of God.
The burden of Tyre: the ships of Tarshish howl, for the great merchant city is laid waste. Her trade is ruined. The wealth of nations cannot stand against the judgment of God.
Zidon is bidden be ashamed, and men ask, is this the joyous, ancient city now brought low? Her glory is gone. Antiquity and renown are no shield when God determines to humble pride.
Zidon is bidden be ashamed, and men ask, is this the joyous, ancient city now brought low? Her glory is gone. Antiquity and renown are no shield when God determines to humble pride.
Zidon is bidden be ashamed, and men ask, is this the joyous, ancient city now brought low? Her glory is gone. Antiquity and renown are no shield when God determines to humble pride.
Zidon is bidden be ashamed, and men ask, is this the joyous, ancient city now brought low? Her glory is gone. Antiquity and renown are no shield when God determines to humble pride.

Who purposed this against the crowning city? 'The LORD of hosts... to stain the pride of all glory.' He is the author of her fall. God lays low the proud to teach that all human honour is His to give and take.

Who purposed this against the crowning city? 'The LORD of hosts... to stain the pride of all glory.' He is the author of her fall. God lays low the proud to teach that all human honour is His to give and take.
The daughter of Tarshish is loosed, and God has stretched His hand over the sea to shake the kingdoms. The maritime power is undone. No realm of commerce is beyond His reach.
The daughter of Tarshish is loosed, and God has stretched His hand over the sea to shake the kingdoms. The maritime power is undone. No realm of commerce is beyond His reach.
The daughter of Tarshish is loosed, and God has stretched His hand over the sea to shake the kingdoms. The maritime power is undone. No realm of commerce is beyond His reach.
The land of the Chaldeans is pointed to as an example; the ships howl, for their stronghold is laid waste. Ruin spreads from one to another. The fall of one proud city warns all the rest.
The land of the Chaldeans is pointed to as an example; the ships howl, for their stronghold is laid waste. Ruin spreads from one to another. The fall of one proud city warns all the rest.

After seventy years Tyre shall trade again, but her gain shall be 'holiness to the LORD,' devoted to His people. Even her commerce is redeemed. God can turn the wealth of nations to the service of His kingdom.
